sourceMovie.title: American Wedding
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: American Wedding works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Amélie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If American Wedding clicked for you, Amélie (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after American Wedding?
faq.answer: Try Amélie, Bridesmaids, Some Like It Hot—each shares DNA with American Wedding while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is American Wedding?
faq.answer: American Wedding (2003) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is American Wedding part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "American Pie Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
